query: We are cutting v0.1.0 — the first public release of our new open-source library. Help me write the release notes in postmortem style.
expected_behavior: Does NOT apply the six-field postmortem structure (no per-fix entries with file/function, root-cause class, or 'test added' fields)

Explains that a first release has no prior regressions to postmortem, so postmortem-style notes do not fit; recommends feature-announcement notes covering capability list, supported platforms, install instructions, and known limitations instead

Hands off to (or describes) a feature-announcement pattern as the correct artifact for v0.1.0, optionally noting that postmortem-style notes become appropriate starting with the first patch or regression-fix release after v0.1.0
